questions.question: or more   2 Which statement about the arthropods is correct? A The same number of insects and crustaceans were counted., B 25% of the arthropods counted were arachnids., C 50% of the arthropods counted were insects., D 10% of the arthropods counted were crustaceans. 3 3   The diagram shows two plant cells, X and Y. Cell X has a higher water potential than Y. X Y In which direction and by which process will water move between these two cells? direction process, A X to Y active transport, B X to Y osmosis, C Y to X active transport, D Y to X osmosis 4   Which row identifies how ions and molecules move by active transport? against the concentration gradient down the concentration gradient through a membrane requiring energy, A     key, B      = yes, C       = no, D     4 5   A student carried out two tests on a liquid. When Benedict’s solution was added to half of the liquid and heated, it turned red. When biuret reagent was added to the rest of the liquid, it turned mauve (purple). Which conclusion is correct?, A The liquid contained solutions of lipids and glucose., B The liquid contained solutions of lipids and protein., C The liquid contained solutions of starch and glucose., D The liquid contained solutions of glucose and protein. 6   Enzymes are involved in many reactions in living cells. Which statement describes the action of enzymes?, A Enzymes are able to start up reactions that would otherwise not occur., B Enzymes can only act inside living cells or organisms., C Enzymes increase the rate of chemical reactions., D One enzyme can catalyse a number of different reactions. 7   The diagram shows the movement of substances involved in photosynthesis in a leaf. 1 5 3 2 4 Which row shows the correct labels for the diagram? 1 2 3 4 5, A carbon dioxide water glucose water oxygen, B oxygen glucose water glucose carbon dioxide, C oxygen water glucose water carbon dioxide, D water glucose carbon dioxide   carbon dioxide oxygen 5 8   The diagram shows a plant cell. P Compared  with  the  rest  of  the  cell,  which  row  describes  the  concentrations  of  oxygen  and magnesium inside structure P during the daytime? oxygen     magnesium, A high high, B high low, C low high, D low low 9   The diagram shows a root hair cell. How does the shape of the cell help the plant?, A It allows roots to search for ions., B It allows roots to search for water., C It increases the surface area for sugar uptake., D It increases the surface area for water uptake. 6 10  A  student  used  the  apparatus  shown  to  measure  the  rate  of  water  loss  by  the  leafy  stem  at increasing wind speeds. leafy stem water air bubble Which graph was produced from their results? wind speed A water loss wind speed B water loss wind speed C water loss wind speed D water loss 7 11  A deficiency disease in children causes soft and deformed bones. What is the name and cause of this deficiency disease? name cause, A rickets lack of vitamin D and calcium, B rickets lack of vitamin C and calcium, C anaemia lack of iron and calcium, D scurvy lack of vitamin C 12  The diagram shows the human digestive system. 1 2 3 Where do egestion and ingestion occur? egestion     ingestion, A 1 3, B 2 3, C 3 1, D 3 2 8 13  Which graph best represents the effect of pH on the action of gastric protease? rate of reaction pH A 114 rate of reaction pH B 114 rate of reaction pH C 114 rate of reaction pH D 114 77 77 9 14  The graph shows the changes in lung volume during breathing when a person was at rest and then when they began to exercise. 6 5 4 3 2 1 0 lung volume / dm 3 0 102030405060708090100110120 time / s at restexercise From  resting  to  exercise,  the  volume  of  air  breathed  in  at  each  breath  increased  from ......x...... dm 3 to ......y...... dm 3 . Looking at the graph, which numbers correctly complete the gaps in the sentence? x   y, A 0.5 3.0, B 0.5 5.0, C 2.0 5.0, D 2.0 5.0 15  What are the products of anaerobic respiration in muscle? alcohol    carbon dioxide    glucose     lactic acid water, A     , B     , C     , D      10 16  Which label shows where lactic acid is broken down? A B D C 17  Two people carried out a short period of exercise on a treadmill. Each of the two people ran on the treadmill for the same amount of time and at the same speed. The  graph  shows  the  pulse  rates  of  these  two  people  before,  during  and  after  the  period  of exercise. pulse rate / beats per minute time / minutes period of exercise person X person Y Which interpretation of the graph is correct?, A Person X is fitter, and their heart rate increases more during exercise., B Person X is fitter, and their heart rate recovers more quickly after exercise., C Person Y is fitter, and their heart rate increases more during exercise., D Person Y is fitter, and their heart rate recovers more quickly after exercise. 18  Which blood vessels carry deoxygenated blood?, A aorta and vena cava, B pulmonary artery and hepatic vein, C renal vein and pulmonary vein, D vena cava and pulmonary vein 11 19  What describes the microorganism that causes cholera and its method of transmission?, A bacteria in contaminated blood, B bacteria in contaminated water, C virus in contaminated blood, D virus in contaminated water 20  The  chart  gives  information  about  a  car  being  driven  at  110 km / h  by  someone  who  has not consumed alcohol. It shows how far the car travels from the time the driver sees a hazard and reacts by using the brakes and how far the car will then travel before finally stopping. 0 102030405060708090100110120 distance travelled / m hazard seen brakes used car stops If the same driver consumes alcohol before driving, the distance travelled before reacting to the hazard increases by 20%. What would then be the total distance travelled by the car before stopping?, A 96 m B 100 m C 111.2 m D 115.2 m

questions.question: Drugs can be used to treat infections in the body. What are antibiotics used for? A to destroy bacteria, B to destroy viruses, C to develop passive immunity, D to stimulate an immune response 12
questions.text: Drugs can be used to treat infections in the body. What are antibiotics used for? A to destroy bacteria, B to destroy viruses, C to develop passive immunity, D to stimulate an immune response 12
options: D to stimulate an immune response 12

questions.question: Human  immunodeficiency  virus  (HIV)  affects  the  immune  system  resulting  in  decreased lymphocyte numbers. The diagnosis of AIDS can be made when lymphocyte numbers decrease below 200 cells / mm 3 of blood. The graph shows the lymphocyte numbers after HIV infection in a patient. 0246810 years after infection lymphocyte count / cells per mm 3 of blood 1200 1000 800 600 400 200 0 How many years after infection would this patient be diagnosed with AIDS? A 0 years, B 3 years, C 7 years, D 9 years
questions.text: Human  immunodeficiency  virus  (HIV)  affects  the  immune  system  resulting  in  decreased lymphocyte numbers. A 0 years, B 3 years, C 7 years, D 9 years

questions.question: Some people require a treatment called dialysis to remove waste products and excess salts from their blood. For these individuals, which part of the body is not functioning correctly? A bladder, B heart, C kidney, D ureter
questions.text: Some people require a treatment called dialysis to remove waste products and excess salts from their blood. For these individuals, which part of the body is not functioning correctly? A bladder, B heart, C kidney, D ureter

questions.question: Which  change  occurs  in  the  eye  as  a  person  focuses  on  a  vehicle  approaching  from  a  long distance? A ciliary muscles relax, B iris circular muscles contract, C lens becomes more convex, D pupil becomes larger 13
questions.text: Which  change  occurs  in  the  eye  as  a  person  focuses  on  a  vehicle  approaching  from  a  long distance? A ciliary muscles relax, B iris circular muscles contract, C lens becomes more convex, D pupil becomes larger 13

questions.question: A light was shone into a person’s eye for 50 seconds. During this time, the intensity of the light was varied. The graph shows changes in the person’s pupil size. 0 1020304050 0 1 2 3 4 5 time / seconds diameter of pupil / mm Which statement explains the change in pupil size? A The light slowly became brighter., B The light suddenly became brighter., C The light slowly became dimmer., D The light suddenly became dimmer. 14
questions.text: A light was shone into a person’s eye for 50 seconds. During this time, the intensity of the light was varied. The graph shows changes in the person’s pupil size. 0 1020304050 0 1 2 3 4 5 time / seconds diameter of pupil / mm Which statement explains the change in pupil size? A The light slowly became brighter., B The light suddenly became brighter., C The light slowly became dimmer., D The light suddenly became dimmer. 14
options: A The light slowly became brighter.
B The light suddenly became brighter.
C The light slowly became dimmer.
D The light suddenly became dimmer. 14

questions.question: The graph shows the blood sugar level for a person over eight hours. They did not exercise during this time. 06:0008:0010:00 time of day 12:0014:00 normal breakfast eaten X Y blood sugar level Which statement explains the shape of the graph between X and Y? A The pancreas produces glucagon which causes excess sugar in the blood to be converted to glycogen., B The  liver  produces  insulin  which  causes  excess  sugar  in  the  blood  to  be  converted  to glycogen., C The pancreas produces insulin which causes excess sugar in the blood to be converted to glycogen., D The  liver  produces  glycogen  which  causes  excess  sugar in  the  blood  to  be  converted  to glucagon.
questions.text: The graph shows the blood sugar level for a person over eight hours. They did not exercise during this time. 06:0008:0010:00 time of day 12:0014:00 normal breakfast eaten X Y blood sugar level Which statement explains the shape of the graph between X and Y? A The pancreas produces glucagon which causes excess sugar in the blood to be converted to glycogen., B The  liver  produces  insulin  which  causes  excess  sugar  in  the  blood  to  be  converted  to glycogen., C The pancreas produces insulin which causes excess sugar in the blood to be converted to glycogen., D The  liver  produces  glycogen  which  causes  excess  sugar in  the  blood  to  be  converted  to glucagon.
options: A The pancreas produces glucagon which causes excess sugar in the blood to be converted to glycogen.
B The  liver  produces  insulin  which  causes  excess  sugar  in  the  blood  to  be  converted  to glycogen.
C The pancreas produces insulin which causes excess sugar in the blood to be converted to glycogen.
D The  liver  produces  glycogen  which  causes  excess  sugar in  the  blood  to  be  converted  to glucagon.

questions.question: Which actions may lead to the contraction of hair erector muscles in the skin? 1   being sprayed with cold water 2   wearing a thick coat 3   entering a hot room 4   entering a cold room A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4 15
questions.text: Which actions may lead to the contraction of hair erector muscles in the skin? 1   being sprayed with cold water 2   wearing a thick coat 3   entering a hot room 4   entering a cold room A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4 15
options: A 1 and 3 B 1 and 4 C 2 and 3 D 2 and 4 15

questions.question: The diagram shows how a large number of carrot plants can be produced from small pieces of carrot root. root of carrot plant each small plant grows into a new carrot plant pieces of carrot root are removed pieces of carrot root are grown into small plants Which row is correct for this method of plant production? asexual reproduction genetically identical offspring cell division by mitosis cell division by meiosis A    , B    , C    , D    

questions.question: In plant reproduction, the following processes occur. 1   fertilisation 2   growth of a pollen tube 3   pollination 4   seed germination In which order do these processes take place? A 1  4  2  3, B 2  1  3  4, C 3  2  1  4, D 4  3  1  2 17

questions.question: In a DNA molecule, bonds between pairs of bases hold the strands together. Which pairs of bases are found in DNA? 1   A-C 2   A-G 3   A-T 4   C-G 5   C-T 6   T-G A 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6, B 1 and 6 only, C 2 and 5 only, D 3 and 4 only
questions.text: In a DNA molecule, bonds between pairs of bases hold the strands together. Which pairs of bases are found in DNA? 1   A-C 2   A-G 3   A-T 4   C-G 5   C-T 6   T-G A 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6, B 1 and 6 only, C 2 and 5 only, D 3 and 4 only

questions.question: The inheritance of the ABO blood groups in humans is controlled by three alleles (I A , I B and I O ), only two of which can be present in one individual. What are the possible blood groups of children born to a homozygous group A woman and a heterozygous group B man? A AB and B only, B AB and A only, C A, B and AB only, D A, B, AB and O 19
questions.text: The inheritance of the ABO blood groups in humans is controlled by three alleles (I A , I B and I O ), only two of which can be present in one individual. What are the possible blood groups of children born to a homozygous group A woman and a heterozygous group B man? A AB and B only, B AB and A only, C A, B and AB only, D A, B, AB and O 19
options: C A, B and AB only
D A, B, AB and O 19

questions.question: What is the definition of a community? A a group of organisms of one species living in an area at the same time, B all the populations of organisms of different species living in an ecosystem, C the number of different species that live in an area, D organisms and their environment interacting together
questions.text: What is the definition of a community? A a group of organisms of one species living in an area at the same time, B all the populations of organisms of different species living in an ecosystem, C the number of different species that live in an area, D organisms and their environment interacting together
options: A a group of organisms of one species living in an area at the same time
B all the populations of organisms of different species living in an ecosystem
C the number of different species that live in an area
D organisms and their environment interacting together

questions.question: The list shows some changes that may occur in a lake that is polluted with nitrogen-containing fertiliser. 1   Concentration of oxygen decreases. 2   Decomposers feed on plants. 3   Green microorganisms grow and cover the surface. 4   Plants die. In which order do these changes occur? A 1  2  3  4, B 2  1  4  3, C 3  4  2  1, D 4  3  1  2
questions.text: The list shows some changes that may occur in a lake that is polluted with nitrogen-containing fertiliser. 1   Concentration of oxygen decreases. 2   Decomposers feed on plants. 3   Green microorganisms grow and cover the surface. 4   Plants die.
